trans-unit.source: Exercise 6: Sava Flood response
trans-unit.target: Exercise 6: Sava Flood response

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: Participants will work with multiple layers and conduct spatial queries. Additionally, they will learn how to create their own geodata.
trans-unit.target: Participants will work with multiple layers and conduct spatial queries. Additionally, they will learn how to create their own geodata.

trans-unit.source: __Type of trainings exercise:__
trans-unit.target: __Type of trainings exercise:__

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: QGIS Essentials
trans-unit.target: QGIS Essentials

trans-unit.source: Working with multiple layers
trans-unit.target: Working with multiple layers

trans-unit.source: Conduct spatial queries
trans-unit.target: Conduct spatial queries

trans-unit.source: Creation of geodata
trans-unit.target: Creation of geodata

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: The exercise takes around 3 hours to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.
trans-unit.target: The exercise takes around 3 hours to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Layer Concept](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Layer Concept](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_layer_concept_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Classification- Categorized](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Classification- Categorized](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Classification - Graduated](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_graduated_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Classification - Graduated](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_graduated_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Spatial Queries](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Spatial Queries](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Table function - Add field](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#add-field)
trans-unit.target: [Table function - Add field](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#add-field)

trans-unit.source: [Digitisation- Point data](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.html#add-geometries-to-a-layer)
trans-unit.target: [Digitisation- Point data](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.html#add-geometries-to-a-layer)

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 1 :::{grid-item-card} __Context:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 1 :::{grid-item-card} __Context:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: "The Government declared a national emergency situation, on 3 April, following the passage of the Tropical Cyclone (TS) Gamane, that hit the north and northeast of Madagascar on 27 March" [Madagascar: Tropical Cyclone Gamane Flash Update No. 2, 4 April 2024 (reliefweb)](https://reliefweb.int/report/madagascar/madagascar-tropical-cyclone-gamane-flash-update-no-2-4-april-2024). The following analysis will utilize actual data from this natural disaster. The objective is to pinpoint the specific medical centers and healthcare facilities that were impacted by the flooding. Additionally, we will assess the viability of road access to the populated places.
trans-unit.target: "The Government declared a national emergency situation, on 3 April, following the passage of the Tropical Cyclone (TS) Gamane, that hit the north and northeast of Madagascar on 27 March" [Madagascar: Tropical Cyclone Gamane Flash Update No. 2, 4 April 2024 (reliefweb)](https://reliefweb.int/report/madagascar/madagascar-tropical-cyclone-gamane-flash-update-no-2-4-april-2024). The following analysis will utilize actual data from this natural disaster. The objective is to pinpoint the specific medical centers and healthcare facilities that were impacted by the flooding. Additionally, we will assess the viability of road access to the populated places.

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module3/Exercise_6/Modul_3_Exercise_6_Sava_flood.zip) and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module3/Exercise_6/Modul_3_Exercise_6_Sava_flood.zip) and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__

trans-unit.source: Dataset name
trans-unit.target: Dataset name

trans-unit.source: Original title
trans-unit.target: Original title

trans-unit.source: Publisher
trans-unit.target: Publisher

trans-unit.source: Download from
trans-unit.target: Download from

trans-unit.source: mdg_admin1.shp
trans-unit.target: mdg_admin1.shp

trans-unit.source: [Subnational Administrative Boundaries](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-mdg)
trans-unit.target: [Subnational Administrative Boundaries](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-mdg)

trans-unit.source: UN OCHA
trans-unit.target: UN OCHA

trans-unit.source: HDX
trans-unit.target: HDX

trans-unit.source: mdg_admin2.shp
trans-unit.target: mdg_admin2.shp

trans-unit.source: [Subnational Administrative Boundaries](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-mdg)
trans-unit.target: [Subnational Administrative Boundaries](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-mdg)

trans-unit.source: UN OCHA
trans-unit.target: UN OCHA

trans-unit.source: HDX
trans-unit.target: HDX

trans-unit.source: hotosm_mdg_health_facilities.gpkg
trans-unit.target: hotosm_mdg_health_facilities.gpkg

trans-unit.source: [Madagascar Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export)]([https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hotosm_pak_health_facilities](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/madagascar-healthsites))
trans-unit.target: [Madagascar Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export)]([https://data.humdata.org/dataset/hotosm_pak_health_facilities](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/madagascar-healthsites))

trans-unit.source: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT)
trans-unit.target: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team (HOT)

trans-unit.source: HDX
trans-unit.target: HDX

trans-unit.source: TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG.shp
trans-unit.target: TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG.shp

trans-unit.source: [Satellite detected water extent over Sambava and Vohemar Districts, Sava Region, Madagascar as of 01 April 2024]([[https://data.humdata.org/dataset/satellite-detected-water-extents-from-08-to-12-august-2024-over-pakistan](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/water-extent-over-sambava-and-vohemar-districts-sava-region-madagascar-as-of-01-april-2024](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/water-extent-over-sambava-and-vohemar-districts-sava-region-madagascar-as-of-01-april-2024)))
trans-unit.target: [Satellite detected water extent over Sambava and Vohemar Districts, Sava Region, Madagascar as of 01 April 2024]([[https://data.humdata.org/dataset/satellite-detected-water-extents-from-08-to-12-august-2024-over-pakistan](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/water-extent-over-sambava-and-vohemar-districts-sava-region-madagascar-as-of-01-april-2024](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/water-extent-over-sambava-and-vohemar-districts-sava-region-madagascar-as-of-01-april-2024)))

trans-unit.source: UNOSAT
trans-unit.target: UNOSAT

trans-unit.source: HDX
trans-unit.target: HDX

trans-unit.source: roads_sava.gpkg
trans-unit.target: roads_sava.gpkg

trans-unit.source: Roads Sava
trans-unit.target: Roads Sava

trans-unit.source: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team
trans-unit.target: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team

trans-unit.source: HOT Export Tool
trans-unit.target: HOT Export Tool

trans-unit.source: :::{hint} Folder structure
trans-unit.target: :::{hint} Folder structure

trans-unit.source: To keep your data organized and easily accessible, it's important to establish a clear folder structure on your computer for your QGIS projects and geodata. Ensure that your exercise data are saved in a location that allows for easy retrieval and association with the corresponding QGIS project.
trans-unit.target: To keep your data organized and easily accessible, it's important to establish a clear folder structure on your computer for your QGIS projects and geodata. Ensure that your exercise data are saved in a location that allows for easy retrieval and association with the corresponding QGIS project.

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Gain an overview of the situation around Sambava and Vehomar
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Gain an overview of the situation around Sambava and Vehomar

trans-unit.source: __Context:__
trans-unit.target: __Context:__

trans-unit.source: You have been deployed as an information manager to the flood-affected regions of Madagascar. Upon your arrival you received reports from the operations team indicating that the distrcits [Sambava and Vohemar](https://www.openstreetmap.org/search?query=Sava%2C%20Madagascar#map=8/-14.374/49.795) of the region Sava are affected by the floods. The team needs a general overview of the affected locations.
trans-unit.target: You have been deployed as an information manager to the flood-affected regions of Madagascar. Upon your arrival you received reports from the operations team indicating that the distrcits [Sambava and Vohemar](https://www.openstreetmap.org/search?query=Sava%2C%20Madagascar#map=8/-14.374/49.795) of the region Sava are affected by the floods. The team needs a general overview of the affected locations.

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`.

trans-unit.source: Once the project is created [save the project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#save) in the "project" folder of the exercise “Module_3_Exercise_2_Flood_Larkana”. To do that click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project "MDG_Sava_flood_2024".
trans-unit.target: Once the project is created [save the project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#save) in the "project" folder of the exercise “Module_3_Exercise_2_Flood_Larkana”. To do that click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project "MDG_Sava_flood_2024".

trans-unit.source: First, we want to add the OpenStreetMap as a base map for orientation. To add the OSM as a base map click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add XYZ Layer…`. Choose `OpenStreetMap` and click `Add`.
trans-unit.target: First, we want to add the OpenStreetMap as a base map for orientation. To add the OSM as a base map click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add XYZ Layer…`. Choose `OpenStreetMap` and click `Add`.

trans-unit.source: Next, load the GeoPackage __"mdg_admin2.gpkg"__ in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to __"mdg_admin2.gpkg"__. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).
trans-unit.target: Next, load the GeoPackage __"mdg_admin2.gpkg"__ in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to __"mdg_admin2.gpkg"__. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).

trans-unit.source: First, we want to export the district __Sambava__ and the neighbouring district __Vohemar__ from __mdg_admin2__ to have it as a stand-alone vector layer. To do that:
trans-unit.target: First, we want to export the district __Sambava__ and the neighbouring district __Vohemar__ from __mdg_admin2__ to have it as a stand-alone vector layer. To do that:

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table of __mdg_admin2__ by right click on the layer → `Open Attribute Table` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html)).
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table of __mdg_admin2__ by right click on the layer → `Open Attribute Table` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html)).

trans-unit.source: Find the row of Sambava in the column __ADM2_EN__ and mark it by clicking on the number on the very left-hand side of the attribute table. The row will appear blue and the area of Sambava will turn yellow on the map canvas. You can right-click on the row and click `Zoom to Feature` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#zoom-in-on-a-specific-feature)). To select the Vohemar district, click on the `Select Feature(s)` ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_selection.png) icon in the QGIS Toolbar, hold the `Shift` button on your keyboard, and click on the districts either on the map or the attribute table ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html#manual-selection)).
trans-unit.target: Find the row of Sambava in the column __ADM2_EN__ and mark it by clicking on the number on the very left-hand side of the attribute table. The row will appear blue and the area of Sambava will turn yellow on the map canvas. You can right-click on the row and click `Zoom to Feature` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#zoom-in-on-a-specific-feature)). To select the Vohemar district, click on the `Select Feature(s)` ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_selection.png) icon in the QGIS Toolbar, hold the `Shift` button on your keyboard, and click on the districts either on the map or the attribute table ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html#manual-selection)).

trans-unit.source: After you are done selecting districts, click on the icon ![](/fig/qgis_move_symbol.png) to end the feature selection mode.
trans-unit.target: After you are done selecting districts, click on the icon ![](/fig/qgis_move_symbol.png) to end the feature selection mode.

trans-unit.source: Now right-click on the layer in the Layer Panel and click on `Export` → `Save Selected Features as`. We want to save the selected districts as a GeoPackage, so choose the `Format` option accordingly. Click on the three points and navigate to your `temp` folder. Here you can give it the layer the name __“Flood_2024_AOI”__ and click `Save`. Now you should see the same name in the `Layer name` field. Click `OK` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_queries_wiki.html#save-selected-features-as-a-new-file)).
trans-unit.target: Now right-click on the layer in the Layer Panel and click on `Export` → `Save Selected Features as`. We want to save the selected districts as a GeoPackage, so choose the `Format` option accordingly. Click on the three points and navigate to your `temp` folder. Here you can give it the layer the name __“Flood_2024_AOI”__ and click `Save`. Now you should see the same name in the `Layer name` field. Click `OK` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_queries_wiki.html#save-selected-features-as-a-new-file)).

trans-unit.source: Click on the icon ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_deselection.png) in the toolbar to end the feature selection.
trans-unit.target: Click on the icon ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_deselection.png) in the toolbar to end the feature selection.

trans-unit.source: Now you have an overview of where the districts Sambava and Vohemar in Sava are located. The operations team can use this information.
trans-unit.target: Now you have an overview of where the districts Sambava and Vohemar in Sava are located. The operations team can use this information.

trans-unit.source: Task 2: Estimation of Flood Impact on the Health Sector in Sambava and Vohemar
trans-unit.target: Task 2: Estimation of Flood Impact on the Health Sector in Sambava and Vohemar

trans-unit.source: __Context:__
trans-unit.target: __Context:__

trans-unit.source: Posts on social media have indicated a significant impact on the healthcare system in Madgascar. You have been tasked to find out as much as you can about the situation in Sava and, if feasible, to estimate the impact on the health system.
trans-unit.target: Posts on social media have indicated a significant impact on the healthcare system in Madgascar. You have been tasked to find out as much as you can about the situation in Sava and, if feasible, to estimate the impact on the health system.

trans-unit.source: The first thing to do is to find out where the health facilities are located in the area. To do that, you do a quick search on HDX. You find the dataset Madagascar Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export). This will do for now.
trans-unit.target: The first thing to do is to find out where the health facilities are located in the area. To do that, you do a quick search on HDX. You find the dataset Madagascar Health Facilities (OpenStreetMap Export). This will do for now.

trans-unit.source: Load the GeoPackage __"hotosm_mdg_health_facilities.gpkg"__ in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to __"mdg_admin2.gpkg"__. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).
trans-unit.target: Load the GeoPackage __"hotosm_mdg_health_facilities.gpkg"__ in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)). Or click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`. Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to __"mdg_admin2.gpkg"__. Select the file and click `Open`. Back in QGIS click `Add` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-layer-tab)).

trans-unit.source: First, we must extract the health facilities in our area of interest. We will use the tool __"Extract by Location"__ to do that.
trans-unit.target: First, we must extract the health facilities in our area of interest. We will use the tool __"Extract by Location"__ to do that.

trans-unit.source: Open the `Processing Toolbox` ([here is how](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html#open-toolbox)) and search for the tool.
trans-unit.target: Open the `Processing Toolbox` ([here is how](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html#open-toolbox)) and search for the tool.

trans-unit.source: As `Input Layer` we will use "hotosm_mdg_health_facilities".
trans-unit.target: As `Input Layer` we will use "hotosm_mdg_health_facilities".

trans-unit.source: For `By comparing to the features from` we use the layer “Flood_2024_AOI”.
trans-unit.target: For `By comparing to the features from` we use the layer “Flood_2024_AOI”.

trans-unit.source: As `Geometric predicate` we use `intersect`.
trans-unit.target: As `Geometric predicate` we use `intersect`.

trans-unit.source: To save the output click on the three points at `Extract (location)` → `Save to GeoPackage` and navigate to your `temp` folder. Save the new layer under the name __“Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI”__. Give the new layer the same `Layer name` and click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: To save the output click on the three points at `Extract (location)` → `Save to GeoPackage` and navigate to your `temp` folder. Save the new layer under the name __“Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI”__. Give the new layer the same `Layer name` and click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Open the Attribute table of the new layer and have a look.
trans-unit.target: Open the Attribute table of the new layer and have a look.

trans-unit.source: Extract by location. :::
trans-unit.target: Extract by location. :::

trans-unit.source: Ok, now we have a good overview of the location of health facilities. We need much better information about the flooded area to identify the health facilities impacted by the flood. Fortunately, the UN has just shared a dataset about the extent of floods. Satellite detected water extent over Sambava and Vohemar Districts, Sava Region, Madagascar as of 01 April 2024.
trans-unit.target: Ok, now we have a good overview of the location of health facilities. We need much better information about the flooded area to identify the health facilities impacted by the flood. Fortunately, the UN has just shared a dataset about the extent of floods. Satellite detected water extent over Sambava and Vohemar Districts, Sava Region, Madagascar as of 01 April 2024.

trans-unit.source: Load the dataset __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG.shp"__ into your QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Load the dataset __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG.shp"__ into your QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Adjust the opacity of the flood layer by right-clicking on layer __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab. Adjusted the opacity to around 60 % by moving the slider.
trans-unit.target: Adjust the opacity of the flood layer by right-clicking on layer __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab. Adjusted the opacity to around 60 % by moving the slider.

trans-unit.source: Once you have loaded the layers in QGIS, you can see that they are correctly displayed. However, upon checking the layer information, you can see that the new layers have a different Coordinate Reference System (CRS). They have the EPSG Code 9707 whereas our project has 4326 ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.html#how-to-check-epsg-code-crs-of-your-qgis-project-and-change-it)).
trans-unit.target: Once you have loaded the layers in QGIS, you can see that they are correctly displayed. However, upon checking the layer information, you can see that the new layers have a different Coordinate Reference System (CRS). They have the EPSG Code 9707 whereas our project has 4326 ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.html#how-to-check-epsg-code-crs-of-your-qgis-project-and-change-it)).

trans-unit.source: Right click on the data layer, click on “Properties”.
trans-unit.target: Right click on the data layer, click on “Properties”.

trans-unit.source: The “Layer Properties” Window of the data layer will open. Click on “Information”.
trans-unit.target: The “Layer Properties” Window of the data layer will open. Click on “Information”.

trans-unit.source: Under the headline “Coordinate Reference System (CRS)” you find all information about the CRS. The most important are:
trans-unit.target: Under the headline “Coordinate Reference System (CRS)” you find all information about the CRS. The most important are:

trans-unit.source: __Name:__ Here you find the EPSG Code.
trans-unit.target: __Name:__ Here you find the EPSG Code.

trans-unit.source: __Unites:__ Here you can find whether it is possible to use meters with this data layer, degrees or latitude and longitude.
trans-unit.target: __Unites:__ Here you can find whether it is possible to use meters with this data layer, degrees or latitude and longitude.

trans-unit.source: This will be a problem as soon as we do something different then just displaying the layers. Since we want to manipulate the layers in the next step we need to reproject them first ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.html#changing-the-projection-of-a-vector-layer)).
trans-unit.target: This will be a problem as soon as we do something different then just displaying the layers. Since we want to manipulate the layers in the next step we need to reproject them first ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.html#changing-the-projection-of-a-vector-layer)).

trans-unit.source: Click on the `Vector` tab -> `Data Management Tools` -> `Reproject Layer` or search for the tool in the `Processing Toolbox`.
trans-unit.target: Click on the `Vector` tab -> `Data Management Tools` -> `Reproject Layer` or search for the tool in the `Processing Toolbox`.

trans-unit.source: As `Input layer` select __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG.shp"__
trans-unit.target: As `Input layer` select __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG.shp"__

trans-unit.source: Select as target CRS/ EPSG-Code __4326__.
trans-unit.target: Select as target CRS/ EPSG-Code __4326__.

trans-unit.source: Save the new file in your `temp` folder by clicking on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to `Reprojected`, specify the file name as __"2024_MinFloodExtend_reprojected"__.
trans-unit.target: Save the new file in your `temp` folder by clicking on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to `Reprojected`, specify the file name as __"2024_MinFloodExtend_reprojected"__.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`

trans-unit.source: Delete the old layer from the layer panel by right click on the layer -> `Remove layer`.
trans-unit.target: Delete the old layer from the layer panel by right click on the layer -> `Remove layer`.

trans-unit.source: Adjust the opacity of the flood layer by right-clicking on layer __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab. Adjusted the opacity to around 60% by moving the slider.
trans-unit.target: Adjust the opacity of the flood layer by right-clicking on layer __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab. Adjusted the opacity to around 60% by moving the slider.

trans-unit.source: We have observed that certain health facilities are located within the flooded area. In order to visualise this information on the map, we plan to include a new attribute called __"affected"__ in the attribute table of __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__. To accomplish this, our first step will involve selecting all the affected health facilities. A new column containing this information is then appended to the __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__ attribute table.
trans-unit.target: We have observed that certain health facilities are located within the flooded area. In order to visualise this information on the map, we plan to include a new attribute called __"affected"__ in the attribute table of __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__. To accomplish this, our first step will involve selecting all the affected health facilities. A new column containing this information is then appended to the __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__ attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Open the `Processing Toolbox` ([here is how](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html#open-toolbox)) and search for the tool __"Select by Location"__.
trans-unit.target: Open the `Processing Toolbox` ([here is how](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html#open-toolbox)) and search for the tool __"Select by Location"__.

trans-unit.source: `Select features from` = __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__.
trans-unit.target: `Select features from` = __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__.

trans-unit.source: As `Geometric predicate` we use `intersect`.
trans-unit.target: As `Geometric predicate` we use `intersect`.

trans-unit.source: For `By comparing to the features from` we use the layer __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG"__.
trans-unit.target: For `By comparing to the features from` we use the layer __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG"__.

trans-unit.source: `Modify current selection by` = `creating new selection`.
trans-unit.target: `Modify current selection by` = `creating new selection`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Selecting the flood affected health facilities. :::
trans-unit.target: Selecting the flood affected health facilities. :::

trans-unit.source: In case you encounter the error:
trans-unit.target: In case you encounter the error:

trans-unit.source: You need to first use the tool __"Fix Geometry"__ before repeating the previously failed step 5 of using the tool __"Select by Location"__.
trans-unit.target: You need to first use the tool __"Fix Geometry"__ before repeating the previously failed step 5 of using the tool __"Select by Location"__.

trans-unit.source: To do so open the `Processing Toolbox` ([here is how](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html#open-toolbox)) and search for the tool __"Fix Geometries"__.
trans-unit.target: To do so open the `Processing Toolbox` ([here is how](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_interface_wiki.html#open-toolbox)) and search for the tool __"Fix Geometries"__.

trans-unit.source: `Input layer` = `TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG`
trans-unit.target: `Input layer` = `TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG`

trans-unit.source: Save the new file in your `temp` folder by clicking on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png), specify the file name as __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG_fix"__.
trans-unit.target: Save the new file in your `temp` folder by clicking on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png), specify the file name as __"TDX_20240401_FloodExtent_SambavaDistrict_MDG_fix"__.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Fixing the geometry :::
trans-unit.target: Fixing the geometry :::

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table of __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__ by right click on the layer → `Open Attribute Table`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html)) and activate the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#change-data-in-the-attribute-table)). Now you are able to edit the data directly in the table.
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table of __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__ by right click on the layer → `Open Attribute Table`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html)) and activate the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#change-data-in-the-attribute-table)). Now you are able to edit the data directly in the table.

trans-unit.source: First, we add a new column with the name __“Flood_affected”__. To do so, click on ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png). In the `Add field` window, you have to add the name and set the `Type` to `Text(string)`. Click `OK` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#add-new-column)).
trans-unit.target: First, we add a new column with the name __“Flood_affected”__. To do so, click on ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png). In the `Add field` window, you have to add the name and set the `Type` to `Text(string)`. Click `OK` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#add-new-column)).

trans-unit.source: Add new column :::
trans-unit.target: Add new column :::

trans-unit.source: Now look for the `Show all Features` option in the lower left corner and click on it. Then, select the option `Show selected features` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#manually-select-features-in-the-attribute-table)). This will filter the table to display only the rows that represent the health facilities directly impacted by the flood. Fortunately, no health facilities are directly affected by the flood.
trans-unit.target: Now look for the `Show all Features` option in the lower left corner and click on it. Then, select the option `Show selected features` ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#manually-select-features-in-the-attribute-table)). This will filter the table to display only the rows that represent the health facilities directly impacted by the flood. Fortunately, no health facilities are directly affected by the flood.

trans-unit.source: If any were affected: Write `Yes` in the __"Flood_affected"__ column.
trans-unit.target: If any were affected: Write `Yes` in the __"Flood_affected"__ column.

trans-unit.source: When you are done, click ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits and switch off the editing mode by again clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#change-data-in-the-attribute-table)).
trans-unit.target: When you are done, click ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits and switch off the editing mode by again clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#change-data-in-the-attribute-table)).

trans-unit.source: Click on the icon ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_deselection.png) in the toolbar to end the feature selection.
trans-unit.target: Click on the icon ![](/fig/selection_toolbar_feature_deselection.png) in the toolbar to end the feature selection.

trans-unit.source: To visualise the enriched data set, we use the function "Categorized Classification" function. This means that we select a column from the attribute table and use the content as categories to sort and display the data ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.html)).
trans-unit.target: To visualise the enriched data set, we use the function "Categorized Classification" function. This means that we select a column from the attribute table and use the content as categories to sort and display the data ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.html)).

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer __"Health_Facilities_Flood_2024_AOI"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.

trans-unit.source: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select “Flood_affected”.
trans-unit.target: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select “Flood_affected”.

trans-unit.source: Further down the window, click on `Classify`. Now you should see all unique values or attributes of the selected “Flood_affected” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on each colour in the central field. Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.
trans-unit.target: Further down the window, click on `Classify`. Now you should see all unique values or attributes of the selected “Flood_affected” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on each colour in the central field. Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.

source.ph: name: Flood affected health facilities classification

target.ph: name: Flood affected health facilities classification

trans-unit.source: Flood affected health facilities classification :::
trans-unit.target: Flood affected health facilities classification :::

trans-unit.source: :::{card} __Achievement:__ We've pinpointed that 3 health facilities have been inundated by the floods. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{card} __Achievement:__ We've pinpointed that 3 health facilities have been inundated by the floods. :::

trans-unit.source: Task 3: Logistical access
trans-unit.target: Task 3: Logistical access

trans-unit.source: Context:
trans-unit.target: Context:

trans-unit.source: The operations team is making plans to deliver much-needed supplies to the affected regions in Sambava and Vohemar. Currently, there is uncertainty about how the supplies can be transported there. The operations team has asked for more information on this topic. They need answers to the following three questions:
trans-unit.target: The operations team is making plans to deliver much-needed supplies to the affected regions in Sambava and Vohemar. Currently, there is uncertainty about how the supplies can be transported there. The operations team has asked for more information on this topic. They need answers to the following three questions:

trans-unit.source: Which roads leading into the affected regions are blocked, and at what specific locations are they blocked?
trans-unit.target: Which roads leading into the affected regions are blocked, and at what specific locations are they blocked?

trans-unit.source: If transporting supplies by road into the region is not feasible, what alternative method could be used to deliver the supplies?
trans-unit.target: If transporting supplies by road into the region is not feasible, what alternative method could be used to deliver the supplies?

trans-unit.source: In order to get a clearer picture, we need to import the road network data for the region into QGIS. Look for the file in the input folder. The road network is initially displayed without showing any road types or other relevant details. We should apply a categorized classification technique only to display the specific roads that we are interested in. ::::
trans-unit.target: In order to get a clearer picture, we need to import the road network data for the region into QGIS. Look for the file in the input folder. The road network is initially displayed without showing any road types or other relevant details. We should apply a categorized classification technique only to display the specific roads that we are interested in. ::::

trans-unit.source: Load the dataset __"roads_sava.gpkg"__ from your input folder into your QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Load the dataset __"roads_sava.gpkg"__ from your input folder into your QGIS.

trans-unit.source: For categorized classification right-click on the layer __"roads_sava"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.html)).
trans-unit.target: For categorized classification right-click on the layer __"roads_sava"__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.html)).

trans-unit.source: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select “highway”.
trans-unit.target: On the top you find a dropdown menu. Open it and choose `Categorized`. Under `Value` select “highway”.

trans-unit.source: Further down the window, click on `Classify`. Now you should see all unique values or attributes of the selected “Flood_affacted” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on the coluors in each row in the central field.
trans-unit.target: Further down the window, click on `Classify`. Now you should see all unique values or attributes of the selected “Flood_affacted” column. You can adjust the colours by double-clicking on the coluors in each row in the central field.

trans-unit.source: Remove the tick from all categories except: `motorway`, `primary`, `secondary`, `trunk`. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_2.png
trans-unit.target: Remove the tick from all categories except: `motorway`, `primary`, `secondary`, `trunk`. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_2.png

trans-unit.source: Road classification :::
trans-unit.target: Road classification :::

trans-unit.source: You have the option to customize the width of the main roads' lines to improve the visualization. Open the Symbology window, then select `Symbol`. In the new window, you can adjust the width of the lines to your preference. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_2_2.png
trans-unit.target: You have the option to customize the width of the main roads' lines to improve the visualization. Open the Symbology window, then select `Symbol`. In the new window, you can adjust the width of the lines to your preference. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_2_2.png

trans-unit.source: Road classification :::
trans-unit.target: Road classification :::

trans-unit.source: Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.

trans-unit.source: To simplify the process, we will visually search for blocked roads and mark them with points. For this purpose, we will create an entirely new point dataset representing blocked roads.
trans-unit.target: To simplify the process, we will visually search for blocked roads and mark them with points. For this purpose, we will create an entirely new point dataset representing blocked roads.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Layer` → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.html#create-a-new-layer)).
trans-unit.target: Click on `Layer` → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer`([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.html#create-a-new-layer)).

trans-unit.source: Under `Database` click on ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to `temp` folder. Give the new dataset the name __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__. Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Under `Database` click on ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to `temp` folder. Give the new dataset the name __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__. Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: `Geometry type`: Select `Point`
trans-unit.target: `Geometry type`: Select `Point`

trans-unit.source: Under `Additional dimension` you should always make sure that you check none of them..
trans-unit.target: Under `Additional dimension` you should always make sure that you check none of them..

trans-unit.source: Select the coordinate reference system (CRS) "EPSG:4326-WGS 84". By default, QGIS selects the project CRS.
trans-unit.target: Select the coordinate reference system (CRS) "EPSG:4326-WGS 84". By default, QGIS selects the project CRS.

trans-unit.source: Under `New Field` you can add columns to the new layer. Add the column __“Blocked_road”__.
trans-unit.target: Under `New Field` you can add columns to the new layer. Add the column __“Blocked_road”__.

trans-unit.source: `Name` = __“Blocked_road”__
trans-unit.target: `Name` = __“Blocked_road”__

trans-unit.source: `Type`: Select `Text (string)`
trans-unit.target: `Type`: Select `Text (string)`

trans-unit.source: Click on `Add to Fields List` ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png) to add the new column to the `Fields List`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Add to Fields List` ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png) to add the new column to the `Fields List`.

trans-unit.source: Create another field with the `name` __"Blocked_bridge"__ and the `Type`: Select `Text (string)`.
trans-unit.target: Create another field with the `name` __"Blocked_bridge"__ and the `Type`: Select `Text (string)`.

trans-unit.source: Click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Your new layer will appear in the `Layer Panel`. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_Task3_3.png
trans-unit.target: Your new layer will appear in the `Layer Panel`. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_Task3_3.png

trans-unit.source: New layer with the blocked roads. :::
trans-unit.target: New layer with the blocked roads. :::

trans-unit.source: Now you can create a point for each place where the flood layer covers the main roads leading through AOI [wiki](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.html#creation-of-point-data). Currently the new layer __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__ is empty. To add features we can use the `Digitizing Toolbar`. If you cannot see the toolbar, click on the tab `View` → `Toolbars` and check `Digitizing Toolbar` ([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.md#creation-of-point-data)). ![](/fig/Digitizing_Toolbar.png)
trans-unit.target: Now you can create a point for each place where the flood layer covers the main roads leading through AOI [wiki](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.html#creation-of-point-data). Currently the new layer __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__ is empty. To add features we can use the `Digitizing Toolbar`. If you cannot see the toolbar, click on the tab `View` → `Toolbars` and check `Digitizing Toolbar` ([Wiki Video](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_digitisation_wiki.md#creation-of-point-data)). ![](/fig/Digitizing_Toolbar.png)

trans-unit.source: Activate the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Activate then the option to add new points by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).
trans-unit.target: Activate the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Activate then the option to add new points by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).

trans-unit.source: Look out for places where the flood layer covers the main roads or bridges. Once you have found one, left-click on the location you want to digitise.
trans-unit.target: Look out for places where the flood layer covers the main roads or bridges. Once you have found one, left-click on the location you want to digitise.

trans-unit.source: Once you click on a place, a window will appear. Indicate that the road is blocked by writing `Yes` in the field `Blocked_road`.
trans-unit.target: Once you click on a place, a window will appear. Indicate that the road is blocked by writing `Yes` in the field `Blocked_road`.

trans-unit.source: Repeat this step with all the locations your can find. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_4.png
trans-unit.target: Repeat this step with all the locations your can find. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_4.png

trans-unit.source: Digitising the blocked roads. :::
trans-unit.target: Digitising the blocked roads. :::

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with digitizing click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with digitizing click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Now, we have mapped all roads in our AOI that are blocked by the flood. We can use icons instead of just points to display the layer __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__ to visualise this fact better [wiki](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_single_symbol_wiki.html).
trans-unit.target: Now, we have mapped all roads in our AOI that are blocked by the flood. We can use icons instead of just points to display the layer __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__ to visualise this fact better [wiki](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_single_symbol_wiki.html).

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer __“MDG_flood_2024_blocked_road”__ in the Layer Panel and click on `Properties`. A new window will open up with a vertical tab section on the left. Navigate to the `Symbology` tab.

trans-unit.source: Keep the `Single Symbol` option. Select any symbol from the list that is appropriate for marking blocked roads (make sure the filter is set to `Favourites` or `All Symbols`).
trans-unit.target: Keep the `Single Symbol` option. Select any symbol from the list that is appropriate for marking blocked roads (make sure the filter is set to `Favourites` or `All Symbols`).

trans-unit.source: Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done, click `Apply` and `OK` to close the symbology window.

trans-unit.source: After you are done, click on the icon ![](/fig/qgis_move_symbol.png) to end the feature selection mode. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_5.png
trans-unit.target: After you are done, click on the icon ![](/fig/qgis_move_symbol.png) to end the feature selection mode. :::{figure} /fig/m3_ex6_qgis_task3_5.png

trans-unit.source: Visualising the blocked roads with icons. :::
trans-unit.target: Visualising the blocked roads with icons. :::

trans-unit.source: The operations team has now all the information they need to plan their logistics. Good Job!
trans-unit.target: The operations team has now all the information they need to plan their logistics. Good Job!
